description:
## Goal

Introduce the off-heap refcounted binary store: a leak-allocated struct with atomic retain/release. No global registry, no mutex — each SharedBin is its own allocation, freed when its refcount hits zero.

## Layout

```rust
#[repr(C)]
pub struct SharedBin {
    pub refcount: AtomicUsize,
    pub bit_len: u64,
    pub bytes: Box<[u8]>,
}
```

## API (runtime/src/shared_bin.rs, new module)

```rust
pub fn shared_bin_alloc(bytes: &[u8], bit_len: u64) -> *mut SharedBin;
pub unsafe fn shared_bin_retain(p: *mut SharedBin);
pub unsafe fn shared_bin_release(p: *mut SharedBin);
```

- `shared_bin_alloc` initialises refcount to 1, copies bytes into a fresh `Box<[u8]>`, leaks the SharedBin, returns the raw pointer.
- `shared_bin_retain` performs `fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed)` on the refcount. Caller already owns at least one reference, so Relaxed is sufficient.
- `shared_bin_release` performs `fetch_sub(1, Ordering::Release)`. If the previous value was 1, performs an `Acquire` fence and reconstructs the Box via `Box::from_raw`, letting it drop and free the bytes plus the SharedBin struct.

This is the canonical Arc pattern.

## Test infrastructure

`#[cfg(test)] static SHARED_BIN_LIVE_COUNT: AtomicUsize` increments on alloc, decrements on free. Test-only helper `shared_bin_live_count() -> usize` for assertions.

## Tests

- alloc → retain → release: refcount climbs and falls; final release frees (live count = 0).
- alloc → release: refcount = 0 immediately, freed.
- Concurrent retain/release from two threads on the same SharedBin (spawn 100 retain/release pairs across two threads, assert final refcount and live count are consistent).
- Bytes are preserved across retain/release.

## Non-changes

- No Heap or fz_value integration in this ticket — pure infrastructure.

## Verification

- New tests pass.
- No fz integration yet; module is dead code consumed by .3.
title: fz-shr.2: SharedBin allocator with atomic refcounts
